meta_title: For Freelancers: email automation to Solve no design skills (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no design skills. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Email Automation to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Master email automation to overcome design limitations. A high-level checklist for developers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship premium client solutions.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting layout engines. Leverage programmatic email generation by combining Antigravity’s templating architecture with Claude Code’s autonomous refactoring. By treating email as modular code rather than design assets, you bypass visual debt entirely, delivering high-conversion, data-driven campaigns that look professionally engineered while requiring zero manual pixel-pushing or design intervention.
content.intro:
The client wants a 'stunning' newsletter, but you’re a developer, not an artist. You find yourself trapped in the purgatory of drag-and-drop builders, wasting hours fighting CSS inconsistencies across Outlook and Gmail. The friction between your technical ability and the demand for aesthetic perfection is the silent killer of freelance profitability.

It’s time to stop designing and start engineering. By shifting to a code-first email infrastructure, you transform design from a bottleneck into a repeatable, automated asset. We are going to build a high-performance email pipeline that leverages AI to handle the aesthetics while you maintain total control over the logic.

steps.title: Define the Modular Schema
steps.description: Establish a JSON-based content schema for your emails. This ensures that your automation logic remains decoupled from the final visual output.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust JSON schema for a modular email system including components for hero sections, CTA blocks, and social proof, ensuring compatibility with MJML.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create email_schema.json based on the modular design pattern'

steps.title: Bootstrap the MJML Architecture
steps.description: Use MJML to handle the complex table-based HTML required for email clients, allowing you to write clean, component-based code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an MJML boilerplate template that supports dynamic injection of JSON content, focused on mobile-first responsiveness and dark-mode compatibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize mjml project structure and create base.mjml'

steps.title: Automate Content Injection
steps.description: Create a script that maps your data schema into the MJML templates, removing the need for manual copy-pasting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Node.js script that parses the email_schema.json and injects the values into the MJML templates using a templating engine like Handlebars.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create build_email.js script to compile MJML with dynamic data'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Review Loops
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform automated accessibility and rendering checks before the code is sent to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a linter configuration for MJML that checks for common email client rendering pitfalls and accessibility compliance (WCAG).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add automated linting to the build pipeline'

steps.title: Deploy the CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your automation to a delivery provider to ensure your code-driven emails hit the inbox every time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a GitHub Actions workflow that triggers the build_email.js script and pushes the generated HTML to the SendGrid API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create .github/workflows/deploy.yml for automated email dispatch'

pro_tips: Always use 'table-layout: fixed' in your CSS to prevent content overflow in legacy Outlook versions.
Host your email images on a high-speed CDN with absolute URLs; relative paths will break in 90% of webmail clients.
Use a 'preheader' text strategy in your JSON schema to increase open rates without changing the design layout.
Test your MJML code against Email on Acid or Litmus via API to catch rendering bugs before the client sees them.
Keep your CSS inline at all times; modern email clients strip out style blocks in the <head> section aggressively.
content.next_steps: Integrate your new email pipeline with a CRM like HubSpot or Postmark to trigger these automated emails based on real-time user behavior events.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting time on design tools and start shipping scalable email infrastructure that makes you look like a full-stack wizard. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
